data.narrative:
SkySafe, a Series B-stage defense and security technology company, has raised over $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $95M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B; the median across this set is $1.2B. The disparity between methods reflects variance in company maturity and market positioning within the cohort.

The triangulated range spans $81M (conservative floor blending both methods) to $1.4B (high case anchored to peer distribution), with a median of $648M. This wide spread reflects material uncertainty: SkySafe's specific revenue multiples, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to peers remain key drivers of valuation placement within this band. The peer-median valuation of $1.2B suggests seasoned Series B companies in defense tech command substantial multiples, though application to SkySafe depends on demonstrable revenue traction and defensibility relative to named comparables.
